tokens: Whenever you're ready, I'll get the tools off of you (that is, off Jim's PLSS).
I'll be packing the ETB in the meantime (as per CDR-39).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) Got to go over and get the engine bell sample (as per LMP-36 and CDR-38).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I guess so; I don't know.
Isn't that part of the closeout?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But I heard you ask him; I didn't hear any answer.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You just should be following your checklist for closeout, now.

tokens: Okay, I'm ready to get the tools off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're plenty comfortable on the time.

tokens: Yeah, well, we ought to get the descent engine sample first.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, well I don't need my bags for that.

tokens: (Holding the SESC at the ready) Okay.

tokens: Need to fill that little jewel.

tokens: Don't spill it, we want to get the top part.

tokens: I can take care of the rest.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Or you can just put it in my bag; that's where it's supposed to go.

tokens: Boy, they sure make them tight.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughs) I was waiting for you to pass me.

tokens: I'll tell you, at the high Sun angle, it's warm isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Sounds okay, Jim, just as clean as possible.

tokens: But we can do that inside.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, here it is, right here.

tokens: I put it on the Hand Tool Carrier.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, rather than waste time out here, I'm just going...

tokens: ...to just leave these all like they are.
I think we can stow them just as well that way.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We ought to put them all over on the MESA table.

tokens: And I've got one little thing to take care of here next on my checklist.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll get the penetrometer drum, so don't worry about that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to take care of that other little item here, which is now due.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Maybe you ought to lift up the front end of the Rover, so you're clearly visible.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's just swing it around...Let me get this side here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's just swing it clockwise for about...Why don't you grab the handle over there and let's just swing it clockwise for about 15 (or) 20 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you've got your big eye there why we have a...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Davy, you're going to have to re-align our antenna, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Clever you should think of that, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I had a subtle reminder (that is, the disappearance of the TV picture on the monitor in the Control Room).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, you should have it, according to my AGC.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, why don't you follow me around to the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just to show that our good (U.S.
Govt.) Postal Service takes care of the mail just about any place in the universe, I have the pleasant task to cancel the...I'll wait till you get around here.
You've got a slow TV today.
Meantime...(Long Pause) Tell me when you get a good picture, Joe, and I'll show you something.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're doing the best we can.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: If you want, Joe, I can help you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Ed's whipping the horses, but it just doesn't work.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll point the TV right at Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: To show that our good Postal Service has deliveries any place in the universe, I have the pleasant task of canceling, here on the Moon, the first stamp of a new issue dedicated to commemorate United States achievements in space.
And I'm sure a lot of people have seen pictures of the stamp.
I have the first one here on an envelope.
At the bottom it says, "United States in Space, a decade of achievement," and I'm very proud to have the opportunity here to play postman.
I pull out a cancellation device.
It says, "August the second, 1971, first day of issue".
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What could be a better place to cancel this stamp than right here at Hadley Rille.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: By golly, it even works in a vacuum.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But it's the first time, so I guess they're just learning.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can put a thumbprint on there, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now, I'll stick this back in our special mail pouch here, and we'll deliver it when we return.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think that's pretty good; after only 10 years, here we are spending 3 days on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're wondering if you could use that to mail home an ounce of rocks, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) And I'll stick this in the ETB.
If you'll hold your camera right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Houston) There's something I think you'll find rather interesting.
Which will only take a minute.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll put this penetrometer drum in the ETB, Dave.

tokens: As well as the Solar Wind.

tokens: I haven't put the Solar Wind in yet; but I will, shortly.

tokens: Joe, I hope you have a good picture there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, in my left hand, I have a feather; in my right hand, a hammer.
And I guess one of the reasons we got here today was because of a gentleman named Galileo, a long time ago, who made a rather significant discovery about falling objects in gravity fields.
And we thought where would be a better place to confirm his findings than on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And so we thought we'd try it here for you.
The feather happens to be, appropriately, a falcon feather for our Falcon.
And I'll drop the two of them here and, hopefully, they'll hit the ground at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Galileo was correct in his findings.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Reading CDR-39) "Penetrometer drum; 70 mm; 500 mm; 16 mm (magazines); maps".
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can get the maps, if you want.
Okay, the solar wind is in there now, Joe.

tokens: Nothing like a little science on the Moon, I always say.

tokens: Okay, Jim, we got it all in the ETB?
I'll seal it up if Joe's happy...Joe, you happy with our ETB now?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, (we'll) be happy if you'd police the area there under the seats and on the console.

tokens: I still want to get that BSLSS bag off.

tokens: And, in particular, Dave, we're looking for mags Tango and Romeo.

tokens: Oh, I'm pretty sure I got them in, Joe.
I was reading all that stuff out to you.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, just check in the seats there and the console, and we're happy.

tokens: (To himself) Shouldn't have tossed the penetrometer (staff)

When one of you takes it into...

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, it looks like you're secure.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave and Jim, for your information...

tokens: Okay, Joe, I'm getting ready to drive.

tokens: And your Nav system should be zeroed, and we're looking for a range/distance reading, which you know.

tokens: Okay, I got that mag off (the LMP Hasselblad), Dave.

tokens: And you're going to bring the

tokens: Dave, are you getting off yet?

tokens: A couple of requests: when you get there, we'd like for you to report on possible dust condition on the battery mirrors, and we'd also like for you to tap the amp-hour meters just to see if the readings change.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, Joe, wait until I get there, will you, please?
I've got to concentrate on driving right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just going to have to repeat it again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No wonder I couldn't find my way.
I was wondering why I wasn't getting anything (on the distance indicator).

tokens: Why don't you drive back fast and reset?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Plenty of time, Dave, and I should have called it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But you know, with the cratering out here, if you're going to see the lift-off, I'm almost tempted just to take a shot up on a rise, here.
If I take it right to the exact spot, I'm afraid that you're not going to see it, Joe, because of the depressions and the hummocky, and everything.
What do you think about that?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I'll just stay out here and put you at about 300 feet, which we are.
There's a nice little rise here.
And I'll point you...You want to be heading 255.
Oh, that's all messed up, too.
I got a good spot for you, Joe.
Joe, what's my relative azimuth of the Sun, right now, with west.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can give you a nice spot here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, it's about, between 30 and 40 degrees.

tokens: Okay, I think I've got a good place for you.

tokens: Tap the amp-hour meters just to see if the readings change.

tokens: (Long Pause) (TV) camera's dusted and the LCRU's dusted.

tokens: Okay, Dave; and the rest of the readings, please.

tokens: 00 on the amps (with the drive motors and TV off), of course; the battery temps, 112 and 119; and motor temps are still off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Set up your circuit breaker configuration (as per CDR-40) and open the batt covers, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Everything is going to be open except Bus A and Bus C and the Aux(illary) circuit breaker.
Bus B is Open; Bus D is Open.
Okay, Joe, all circuit breakers are now open with the exception of Aux, Bus A, and Bus C.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Roger, Dave, and Power, External; TV, Remote (as per CDR-41).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm in the middle of getting your (battery) covers, here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And comment on the dust on the battery mirrors.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, there's a little dust on the central mirror.
But the other two seem to be fairly clean.
I just happen to have a camera, which I'll take a picture for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: So you'll know what everything looks like.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm going to Power...(Brief static) There's so much dust on here.
The down position is Remote, right, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's in a shadow and I can't see.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'd like for you to align the high-gain (antenna) per the checklist procedures, but we're standing by for your readings on how possible you think that is, because of the dim intensity of the Earth.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (No answer) Hadley Base, do you read Houston?
(No answer) Hadley Base, do you read Houston?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I guess we're standing by for your high-gain alignment per the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Transferred a few bags up to the porch (by hand).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We have about three more to transfer up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) And, Dave, you might want to check TV Remote.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, give me a call on your present activity.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, just cleaning up the back of the Rover, here, a little, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And, Dave, we do not have our TV yet.

transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) It's sure hard to see the Earth.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You might want to verify the Aux circuit breaker in.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The Aux circuit breaker is in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But you probably aren't getting it because I haven't found the Earth yet.
Looking into the Sun, it's just tough, when you're trying to align this thing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want me to go out there and see if I can assist in any way, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to try it from the other side, now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, maybe the best idea is just to use the AGC technique.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Just hang right there, Jim, for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you can use that background sound, too.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Can't get my visor (close enough to the sighting scope).
There, I think I might be able to find him.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you want, I can come out and watch the AGC meter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Joe, you should be aligned.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll wait till you give me a go.
Make sure you've got what you need.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, cycle the switch: Internal and External once for us, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Joe, let me verify that the position of the Earth in the grid is the important thing and not the angle at which the grid intersects the local vertical.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, that's more or less correct.
I think we're satisfied with the alignment here.
We just need some help on tilting our camera up to the level.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, we need some help bumping our camera up, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I guess our only other request...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I'm going to take one last pan here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we may have overshot on the camera.
We might have to have some help getting it back towards the horizontal.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: One last comment on the mountain that's south of Hadley.
I can see some large outcrops on the upper slopes, on the upper 10 percent.
And they really stand out and there's a talus downslope.
As a matter of fact, it almost looks like we have some layering on the upper slopes, the upper 10 percent, apparently...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Cutting Dave off) Okay, Dave, we copy that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Lost under Joe) you're only up about five degrees.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're interested in moving on back towards the LM.

tokens: That last time you called, the tilt-up was only up about 10 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think Ed (Fendell) must have lost control.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, Ed's been doing pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: He has a pretty high batting average.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, we're ready for you to move the baggage up into Falcon and climb in.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: As the space poet Rhysling (the blind poet in Robert Heinlein's ) would say, we're ready for you to "come back again to the homes of men on the cool green hills of Earth."
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're ready, too, but it's been great.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (At the MESA) Dave, I'm going to start getting in.

tokens: Let me brush you off maybe a little, partner.

tokens: (Pause) Man, that stuff really accumulates, doesn't it?.

tokens: Jim, while you're dusting there, how many suitcases have you carried up?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Garbled) dirty (To Joe) Oh, I only have two up there.

tokens: ...and the core stem up there on the porch...

tokens: Man, I'm glad we got that core!

tokens: Okay, Dave, I think that's as good as we're going to get you.

tokens: Let's take that along and...Oh, here's another (16-mm) mag.

tokens: (Going to the ladder) Want to use the
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think...Oh, I think it'll be okay, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) I think with a little practice, Jim, I could jump all the way to the porch!

tokens: And if you'll...(When you) come up, if you hand me that tool, it'll help.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Here's the ETB; let me get you the tool.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (I'll) go back down and get the others.
(Long Pause, probably as Dave goes down the ladder and then back up) Right over on your right there, partner.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Getting the last one right now, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, just a reminder on the stems there.

tokens: Let me get you this bag.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Those are good little holders, there.

tokens: The caps are on not very tight; so be careful.

tokens: Lie it on the floor, here.

tokens: Why don't you put it back up in the...That's all right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I'm afraid it'll get bumped there, Dave.

tokens: I guess we'll just have to be careful and not step on the thing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, let me...Hold on, Dave, let me...

tokens: I'll get behind my...(Get behind) the hatch.

tokens: I think you can get in now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...We're hoping you've got four collection bags and an ETB...

tokens: We've even got the core stems.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave and Jim, I've noticed a very slight smile on the face of the Professor.
I think you very well may have passed your final exam.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: A little more to your left, if you can.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You're hung up a little on me.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just don't let me get that cover (?).

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now I'll get you to get mine, then.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If you can turn around...Okay, back into your corner.
Let me get back into my corner.
Now I think you can turn around.

tokens: (I'm) thinking maybe I could shift your PLSS some.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can't get down that low.
Go ahead and repress with it (meaning the primary water valve) open.

tokens: (To Jim) Get back in your corner if you can.
You have to turn right so you can get the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I've already got it; (it's) in Auto.

tokens: Okay, just move back so I can get the door then.

tokens: Go back in my little corner?

tokens: Well, we'll check it here on the repress.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...we'd like for you to check it now.

tokens: Okay; get in your corner, Jim; I'll get you from the rear.
Turn around and get in your corner.
Go back to your little corner.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You must have got it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I've got an A flag.

tokens: Dave and Jim, this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; A and B are in Cabin.

tokens: Ed's coming on the line down here.

tokens: Appreciate you keeping such good track of us.

tokens: Wouldn't have missed it for anything.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hadley Base, this is Flight, too.
The whole Mission Control team wants to take their hats off to you for a fine job.
It was a lot of fun.
transcript.speaker: Griffin

tokens: We'd like to take our hats off to the whole team.
By golly, you guys are really sharp down there, and we sure appreciate it.
'Cause you know as well as we do we sure couldn't do it without you.
Cabin pressure's stable at about 4.5." "Use purge valve to depress." We're depressed.
transcript.speaker: Scott
